name: Ink & Light

animation.approach: Spring physics on interactions, gentle content reveals with slight scale (1.02→1.0). Organic, not mechanical.
animation.microInteractions: CSS spring transitions on hover/focus. Scale + opacity on buttons (0.98 press, 1.02 hover).
animation.scrollReveals: fade-up (y:24→0, opacity:0→1, scale:0.98→1), stagger 80ms between siblings. Ease: [0.25, 0.4, 0, 1].

spacing.note: Generous spacing — content-heavy pages need room to breathe

requiredSurprises: Cabinet Grotesk 800 at text-6xl+ creates natural visual weight
Amber-400 accent appears exactly ONCE in the workflow case study aha moment
Product showcase panels break the centered-text rhythm with full-width interactive content
Architecture diagram uses asymmetric hub-and-spoke layout
atmosphereEffects: Subtle noise texture overlay (1-2% opacity, warm grain not digital noise)
Soft radial gradient on hero only (purple-600/5 to transparent)
NO dot grids, NO line grids, NO glow effects — restraint is the aesthetic
forbidden: Violet/purple glow effects (this was Electric Depth's problem)
More than 2 atmosphere layers on any section

components.cards: bg-[#161616] border border-white/[0.08] rounded-xl hover:border-white/[0.14] hover:bg-[#1c1c1c] transition-all duration-200
components.badges: bg-purple-400/10 text-purple-400 text-xs font-medium px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full

buttons.primary: bg-stone-100 text-[#0c0c0c] hover:bg-white font-medium rounded-lg px-5 py-2.5 transition-colors
buttons.secondary: border border-white/[0.08] text-stone-100 hover:border-white/[0.14] hover:bg-[#161616] font-medium rounded-lg px-5 py-2.5 transition-all

components.headline: font-display font-bold text-stone-100 tracking-tight

nanobananaPrefix: Warm dark editorial interface, near-black background, warm stone-white typography, subtle purple accent, clean professional aesthetic, minimal decoration, confident restraint, no glow effects
geminiSvgStyle: Warm minimal, smooth spring easing, purple-400 accent on dark, organic motion, no sharp geometric patterns, editorial confidence
